matches.rationale: Same Bash trigger and same prohibition on direct host pytest, Python, or Airflow commands.
Same Edit target: adding assert in production Python code.
Same Edit target and same requirement to use monotonic time for durations.
Same Edit target: new direct AirflowException raises, with the same prescribed use of narrower exceptions.
Same Edit target: core API route exception translation to HTTPException with 404/400 status.
Same Write target: newly created repository source files require the Apache header.
Same Write target: newsfragments in consuming distributions only for certainly user-facing changes.
Same Write target and same prohibition for provider and airflow-ctl newsfragments.
Same Edit target: Python tests using unittest.TestCase instead of pytest patterns.
Same Edit target: test mocks should use spec or autospec.
Same Edit target: tests checking caplog rather than behavior.
Same Bash commit trigger and the closest extracted rule for user-impact-focused plain commit subjects.
Same Bash commit trigger and same requirement to run prek install.
Same Bash commit trigger and same ban on self/agent Co-Authored-By attribution.
Same Bash push target and same prohibition on upstream or main pushes.
Same Bash remote-operation target and requirement to inspect naming before push or PR creation.
Same Bash PR workflow target and requirement for self-review before submission.
Same Bash push trigger and requirement to rebase on the latest target branch first.
Same Bash PR-creation target and prefilled body with checked AI disclosure.
Same Bash PR-creation target and title constraints: short, imperative, and user-impact focused.
Same Bash issue-creation target and same instruction to skip a redundant issue when a PR is imminent.
Same Bash PR target: deferred or partial work must already have a tracking issue.
Same Edit target and requirement for a full tracking-issue URL rather than a bare number.
Same deferred-work content target and prohibition on promising an issue later rather than linking one now.
Same Bash GitHub-post target and attribution-footer requirement.
Same Bash GitHub-message target and prohibition on unauthorized individual mentions.
Same Edit target and prohibition on modifying the apache-magpie snapshot directly.
Same Bash target and prohibition on destructive git operations without an explicit request.

Compiler explicitly classified scheduler execution behavior as descriptive architecture rather than a tool-bound action.
Compiler explicitly classified worker DB/API access as descriptive architecture rather than a tool-bound action.
The distribution dependency declaration is embedded in architecture description and has no extracted Edit rule.
Compiler explicitly reported security vulnerability-classification guidance as reasoning guidance not tied to a tool call.
Compiler explicitly reported security hardening classification as reasoning guidance not tied to a tool call.
The extracted ruff rules use Edit and Write, so neither can match this Bash rule under the same-tool requirement.
Compiler explicitly reported comment-sparingly guidance as general coding style and extracted no matching Edit rule.
The extracted rule only forbids session.commit; it omits the gold rule's keyword-only session requirement, so the compound rule is not fully covered.
Compiler explicitly reported import placement and its exceptions as general style guidance.
Compiler explicitly reported heavy type-only import guarding as judgment-heavy with no reliable trigger.
The extracted bulk-write rule omits the gold rule's required indexed cleanup filters, so strict coverage is incomplete.
Compiler explicitly reported action-verb function naming as general naming style.
The extracted selective-checks rule requires the documentation update but omits the gold rule's same-PR test update.
No extracted Write rule covers routing task-sdk user-visible notes to airflow-core newsfragments.
Compiler explicitly reported exact changed-behavior coverage as judgment-based test-scope guidance.
The extracted patch-decorator rule omits the gold rule's conf_vars requirements and conditional decorator/context-manager behavior.
The extracted rule only bans datetime.now and does not require time_machine, so it does not cover the complete gold rule.
Compiler explicitly reported parametrizing similar tests as style and judgment guidance.
Compiler explicitly reported db_test marking as requiring semantic judgment about database access.
Compiler explicitly reported source-mirroring test locations as a descriptive convention.
Compiler explicitly reported that no trigger separates generated reports or scratch output from ordinary writes.
No extracted Bash rule targets commit-message bodies or the why-versus-what distinction.
No extracted rule covers re-inspecting remotes specifically after a rename or addition.
No extracted rule covers translating stale remote names and flagging stale documentation.
Compiler explicitly reported the existing-PR check as occurring before a bindable tool call.
The actionable checklist depends on reading referenced code-review instructions; no extracted rule covers that reference-depth requirement.
The combined pre-push checks are spread through a deep numbered self-review checklist and were not extracted.
No extracted rule covers a brief PR description plus applicable issue references.
The tracking-issue content requirements are a multi-part nested list and were not extracted.
The already-open-PR repair sequence is a multi-step structured workflow and was not extracted.
No extracted rule distinguishes unapproved drafts or requires the no-human-review footer variant.
The snapshot-population requirement only becomes actionable through the referenced apache-magpie workflow.
Compiler explicitly reported the magpie review skill choice as a workflow/skill decision rather than a tool-call trigger.
Compiler explicitly reported asking before large cross-package refactors as too vague; the two extracted ask-before rules cover only dependency and migration subsets.
The extracted secret rule uses Edit, while the gold rule uses Bash; same-tool matching therefore fails.
The extracted rule covers only uv.lock, not the gold rule's general target of every generated file with an available workflow.

unmatched_extracted.note: Real Edit-bound ruff requirement; gold represents the operation as Bash, preventing a match.
Real Write-bound ruff requirement; gold represents the operation as Bash, preventing a match.
Only a fragment of the compound gold requirement was extracted, omitting keyword-only session parameters.
Write-tool duplicate of the AirflowException prohibition already matched through the closer Edit rule.
Incomplete extraction of the documented rule because it omits the required indexed cleanup filters.
Incomplete extraction: it omits the required test changes.
Captures only the patch-decorator fragment of a broader gold rule that also governs conf_vars.
Captures the prohibition but omits the affirmative time_machine requirement.
Covers a second facet of g65 after the closest commit-style extraction was used for its one allowed match.
Real Edit-bound secret detection rule; gold models the prohibition as Bash, so same-tool matching fails.
Real specific rule for uv.lock, but it does not cover gold's general generated-file rule.
Real action-bindable subset requiring confirmation for broad-impact dependency changes.
Real action-bindable subset requiring confirmation for destructive migration changes.
